operating_state: No specific revenue or segment performance data provided in the filing excerpts. The company reported Q1 2024 results on February 7, 2024, but detailed financial metrics were not included in the available filing text.
cost_structure: The company expects to record non-cash pre-tax impairment charges of $1.8 billion to $2.4 billion in the current quarter related to the Star India transaction, with approximately half reflecting write-down of Star India net assets and half reflecting goodwill impairment at the entertainment linear networks reporting unit.
material_events: Disney's Board approved a new share repurchase program for up to 400 million shares on February 7, 2024, targeting $3 billion in aggregate repurchases during fiscal 2024. On February 28, 2024, Disney entered binding agreements to form a joint venture combining Star India with Reliance Industries' Viacom18, with Disney retaining 37% ownership and RIL investing $1.4 billion at closing. Disney renewed its credit facilities on March 1, 2024, maintaining $5.25 billion in 364-day capacity and $3 billion in five-year capacity.
macro_exposures: Florida legislative and regulatory actions have negatively impacted Disney's ability to execute business strategy and could affect costs and growth of Florida operations. Enhanced U.S. trade restrictions and sanctions create operational disruptions for international theme parks in France, mainland China and Hong Kong. Various jurisdictions now require specified local content production investments and programming quotas for linear networks and streaming services.
forward_outlook: The Star India joint venture transaction is expected to close in the first half of fiscal 2025, subject to regulatory approvals. The impairment charges will be excluded from adjusted earnings per share and do not impact fiscal 2024 adjusted EPS guidance. Disney may contribute additional media assets to the joint venture pending regulatory and third-party approvals.
uncertainty_profile: Star India will remain subject to held-for-sale accounting adjustments until transaction closing, potentially generating incremental gains or losses from operating results or foreign exchange changes. The joint venture closing remains contingent on regulatory approvals across multiple jurisdictions. Ongoing regulatory developments may impose additional compliance costs or restrict profitable service offerings across Disney's global operations.
